summary: Created complete Morning Briefing Agent system with Python module (morning_briefing.py) and agent instruction (morning-briefing-prompt.md). Module includes get_todays_tasks() with priority ordering (strategic > external > urgent > normal), check_calendar_blocks() for meeting time awareness, generate_briefing_text() for SMS formatting, parse_adjustment() and process_adjustment() for handling V's replies, and lock_day_plan() for commitment tracking. All functions tested and working. Calendar integration returns placeholder data (OAuth pending). Successfully tested task retrieval, briefing generation, adjustment parsing, push/remove/swap/add commands, and day plan locking.

notes_for_orchestrator.setup_instructions:
Create scheduled agent with:
  - RRULE: FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=8;BYMINUTE=0
  - Delivery: sms
  - Instruction: Load from /home/workspace/N5/agents/morning-briefing-prompt.md
  
Agent is ready to create after V reviews the prompt.

integration_points.calendar: check_calendar_blocks() returns placeholder data - OAuth setup needed
integration_points.database: Reads/writes to tasks.db, creates day_plans table if needed

test_results.task_retrieval: ✅ PASS - Retrieved 5 tasks with correct priority ordering
test_results.calendar_check: ✅ PASS - Returns meeting count and available hours
test_results.briefing_generation: ✅ PASS - Generated 382-char briefing with task list
test_results.adjustment_parsing: ✅ PASS - Parsed all commands: confirm, push, swap, remove, add
test_results.adjustment_processing: ✅ PASS - Successfully pushed task to tomorrow
test_results.day_plan_locking: ✅ PASS - Created day_plans table and locked plan

notes_for_orchestrator.priority_order: strategic → external → urgent → normal (within bucket: overdue first, then newest)
notes_for_orchestrator.capacity_heuristic: 8 hrs - meeting hours = available hrs × 0.8 utilization = recommended tasks (clamped to 3-10)
supported_adjustments: push #N [to tomorrow] - Move task to tomorrow
add: task title - Add new task to today
considerations_for_other_drops: D2.3 (evening accountability) should read from day_plans table to compare committed vs completed
Calendar OAuth setup is a dependency for full functionality
SMS responses trigger the same agent - needs to distinguish initial briefing vs adjustment handling
Priority order matches D1.1's view: urgent first in SQL, but morning briefing shows strategic first (user preference)
database_changes: Creates day_plans table if not exists (plan_date, task_ids, total_tasks, created_at, locked_at)
Updates task due_at dates when pushing to tomorrow
Clears due_at when removing from today
